body:
## Summary
- add a new explicit route `add-rubrics` (plus `agent/add-rubrics` label mapping) in triage/dispatch policy
- add runtime support for the route in envelope validation and prompt mapping
- add a dedicated workflow: `.github/workflows/agent-add-rubrics.yml`
  - runs prompt `agent-add-rubrics.md`
  - mounts rubrics with write enabled (`rubrics_mode_override: 'enabled'`)
  - validates/commits through existing `run-agent-task` plumbing
  - posts an in-thread summary comment
- add `.agent/src/cli/prepare-add-rubrics-summary.ts` and response formatter for concise status comments
- update docs and route lists to include `/add-rubrics`

## Verification
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/triage.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/rubrics.test.js`

Closes #9

comments.body:
## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 94 | PARTIAL | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | New `agent-add-rubrics.yml` reuses existing plumbing (`resolve-agent-provider`, `setup-agent-runtime`, `run-agent-task`, `post-response`) rather than introducing new orchestration logic. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Route/workflow docs updated across `.agent/docs/*`, `AGENT.md`, and `README.md` to include `/add-rubrics` and `agent/add-rubrics`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR implements the linked `Closes #9` plan (explicit route, dedicated workflow, summary comment path, tests), matching issue discussion constraints. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Documentation changes add route/workflow behavior without introducing per-file inventory tables. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to route wiring, prompt/workflow, summary formatter CLI, tests, and route docs directly required by the request. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | partial | 4/7 | Summary posting is gated by `steps.add_rubrics.outputs.response_file != ''`; if run fails before producing `response_file`, in-thread status can be skipped (logs still exist). |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Implementation follows existing patterns with minimal new logic: one formatter, one summary CLI, and route additions in existing dispatch/runtime maps. |

## Notes

- Strongest score drivers: high reuse of existing workflow/action architecture, and thorough doc/test updates aligned with the requested behavior.
- Smallest useful follow-up: make failure summary posting independent of `response_file` so failed `add-rubrics` runs always leave an in-thread status.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `agent-add-rubrics.yml` currently posts summary only when `response_file` exists; this can miss user-visible failure status for early/stateful failures (rubric: `surface-stateful-failures`).

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis > Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**. > Requested by @lolipopshock. ## Summary of PR/Issue - This PR adds a new explicit `add-rubrics` route so users can request rubric updates directly from conversation text. - It wires the route through triage/dispatch, envelope validation, runtime prompt mapping, and label handling (`agent/add-rubrics`). - It introduces a dedicated workflow (`agent-add-rubrics.yml`) that runs the new prompt, enables rubric writes, and posts a summary comment. - It also adds a new summary CLI/formatter path and updates route/workflow documentation across the repo. - The scope matches issue [#9](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/9) for a minimal end-to-end implementation. ## Review - Overall implementation direction is correct and aligned with the issue plan, but there are a few correctness/robustness gaps to fix before merge. - Reviewer inputs were mostly consistent; no material disagreement on the main warnings. | Issue | Severity | Description | | -- | -- | -- | | Rubrics schema doc example is malformed | WARNING | In [`.agent/docs/architecture/rubrics.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/5c0be34e4535799b6dfc1933a63692ca153206a6/.agent/docs/architecture/rubrics.md#L36), `applies_to` indentation is broken, making the YAML example invalid/misleading for copy-paste. | | Add-rubrics lane name is inconsistent | WARNING | In [`.github/workflows/agent-add-rubrics.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/5c0be34e4535799b6dfc1933a63692ca153206a6/.github/workflows/agent-add-rubrics.yml#L165), `lane: add` diverges from route-name conventions and can create namespace ambiguity. | | Missing direct tests for `formatAddRubricsComment` | WARNING | [`.agent/src/response.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/5c0be34e4535799b6dfc1933a63692ca153206a6/.agent/src/response.ts#L165) adds new branching formatter behavior, but `response.test.ts` has no focused coverage for its three output branches. | | Stateful failure visibility is incomplete for summary posting | WARNING | Summary preparation is gated on `response_file` existence, so some early/stateful failures can finish without a user-facing status comment even though this is a write workflow. | | Minor comment/documentation consistency nits | INFO | Router section comment label and one prompt schema comment are slightly inconsistent with the new route naming; low risk but worth cleanup. | ## Progress - Existing PR discussion already includes a rubric-review warning about stateful failure visibility. - New inline comments were posted for the three concrete line-level gaps above: - [rubrics schema indentation](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/15#discussion_r3155796435) - [lane naming](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/15#discussion_r3155796524) - [missing formatter tests](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/15#discussion_r3155796616) ## Issue Details
Rubrics schema doc example is malformed **Cause:** A doc edit changed YAML indentation for `applies_to`, making the schema snippet structurally invalid. **Candidate solutions:** Restore `applies_to:` to top-level and keep `- implement ...` indented beneath it (matching other rubric schema examples). **Comments:** Multiple reviewers independently flagged this as misleading for users.
Add-rubrics lane name is inconsistent **Cause:** New workflow uses `lane: add` instead of the established full-route lane naming pattern. **Candidate solutions:** Change to `lane: add-rubrics` for consistency and safer session/thread namespacing. **Comments:** No opposing reviewer rationale was provided for abbreviation.
Missing direct tests for formatAddRubricsComment **Cause:** New formatter was added, but tests cover only neighboring formatters and route wiring. **Candidate solutions:** Add focused unit tests in `response.test.ts` for failed run, committed update, and no-commit-success paths. **Comments:** This is user-visible output, so regression risk is practical rather than theoretical.
Stateful failure visibility is incomplete for summary posting **Cause:** Summary generation depends on `response_file`; some failure modes can skip posting a status comment. **Candidate solutions:** Decouple summary posting from `response_file` presence and include explicit persistence/commit-failure signaling in the formatter inputs. **Comments:** Already surfaced in prior PR discussion; still unresolved.
Minor comment/documentation consistency nits **Cause:** One router section comment and one prompt schema comment were not fully updated to the new route terminology. **Candidate solutions:** Update route comment label and include `add-rubrics` in the prompt schema comment’s allowed values list.
## Recommended Next Step `FIX_PR`: unresolved warnings are concrete branch changes with clear, low-risk fixes suitable for an automated fix pass. ## Final Verdict `MINOR_ISSUES` ## Action Items - [ ] Fix YAML indentation in the rubrics schema example so `applies_to` is valid top-level YAML. - [ ] Change `lane: add` to `lane: add-rubrics` in `agent-add-rubrics.yml`. - [ ] Add unit tests for `formatAddRubricsComment` covering failed, committed, and no-change-success branches. - [ ] Ensure add-rubrics summary posting still surfaces clear status on early/stateful failures (including commit/push failure cases).

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-9/codex-25065166075`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

- Addressed the latest actionable PR feedback from review synthesis with a minimal patch.
- Fixed malformed YAML schema example indentation in `.agent/docs/architecture/rubrics.md` (`applies_to` restored to top-level).
- Updated `.github/workflows/agent-add-rubrics.yml` to use `lane: add-rubrics` for route-consistent namespacing.
- Made add-rubrics summary preparation always run (`if: always()`), so early/stateful failures still produce a user-facing status comment.
- Added focused unit coverage for `formatAddRubricsComment` in `.agent/src/__tests__/response.test.ts` for:
  - failed run (`!runSucceeded`)
  - committed update (`runSucceeded && rubricsCommitted`)
  - no-change success (`runSucceeded && !rubricsCommitted`)

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/triage.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/rubrics.test.js`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/response.test.js`

All passed.
</details>

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 98 | PARTIAL | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | `agent-add-rubrics.yml` reuses existing workflow plumbing: `resolve-github-auth`, `resolve-agent-provider`, `setup-agent-runtime`, `run-agent-task`, and `post-response`; formatter logic lives in `.agent/src/response.ts` with a small CLI wrapper. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | partial | 6/7 | Route lists and workflow docs were updated across `.agent/docs/*`, `AGENT.md`, `README.md`, prompt route lists, and action input docs. One remaining inconsistency: the new `.github/prompts/agent-add-rubrics.md` rubric schema comment omits `add-rubrics` from allowed `applies_to` routes while other schema examples include it. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The PR follows issue #9’s plan: dedicated `/add-rubrics` route, direct `agent/rubrics` update workflow instead of PR flow, validation/commit through existing plumbing, in-thread summary, route tests, and docs. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Documentation additions describe route/workflow behavior and route lists without adding stale per-file inventory tables. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is scoped to add-rubrics route wiring, envelope/runtime mapping, workflow/prompt, summary formatter/CLI, tests, and related docs. No broad refactors or unrelated cleanup are present. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | The workflow now runs `Prepare add-rubrics summary` with `if: always()` and posts when a body file is produced, so early failures still surface a status comment; unreadable response files are logged by `prepare-add-rubrics-summary.ts`. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation is minimal: one route addition through existing maps, one dedicated workflow, one prompt, one formatter, and focused tests. |

## Notes

- The PR now satisfies the earlier stateful-failure rubric concern: summary preparation no longer depends on `response_file` existing.
- Smallest useful follow-up: update `.github/prompts/agent-add-rubrics.md` so its schema comment includes `add-rubrics` in the allowed `applies_to` list.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `.github/prompts/agent-add-rubrics.md` still shows an allowed-route schema comment that excludes `add-rubrics`, even though the PR adds `add-rubrics` to runtime validation and other rubric schema docs. This is a small docs/prompt consistency miss tied to `keep-docs-in-sync`.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis > Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**. > Requested by @lolipopshock. ## Summary of PR/Issue - This PR adds a minimal end-to-end `add-rubrics` route for turning user requests into durable rubric updates. - It wires the route through triage, dispatch, envelope validation, runtime prompt selection, label handling, and documentation. - It adds a dedicated `agent-add-rubrics.yml` workflow that writes directly to `agent/rubrics`, validates rubric edits, commits through existing plumbing, and posts an in-thread summary. - The implementation follows the linked issue #9 direction: direct rubrics-branch updates rather than opening PRs by default. ## Review - The PR is directionally solid and has already addressed several earlier review findings, but the new write workflow still has two operational robustness gaps. - Reviewers agree the remaining issues are fixable without redesign; no meaningful reviewer disagreement was present. | Issue | Severity | Description | | -- | -- | -- | | Add-rubrics writes can race on `agent/rubrics` | WARNING | The new workflow writes directly to the shared rubrics branch but has no concurrency group, so simultaneous `/add-rubrics` runs can race and lose one update. | | Commit/push failures can be summarized as no changes | WARNING | Rubric commit failures are `continue-on-error` inside `run-agent-task`, so the add-rubrics summary can report “No changes were committed” instead of surfacing a persistence failure. | | Prompt schema comment omits `add-rubrics` | WARNING | `.github/prompts/agent-add-rubrics.md` still excludes `add-rubrics` from the example `applies_to` allowed route list, despite runtime/docs now accepting it. | ## Progress - Previously flagged YAML indentation, lane naming, missing formatter tests, and early summary posting gaps were fixed in the PR branch. - Existing discussion already acknowledges the prompt schema omission in the latest rubrics review. - New inline comments were posted for the two concrete workflow issues: - https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/15#discussion_r3160750540 - https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/15#discussion_r3160751124 ## Issue Details
Add-rubrics writes can race on agent/rubrics **Cause:** The workflow writes directly to a shared branch without serializing runs. **Candidate solutions:** Add workflow or job-level `concurrency` scoped to the repository and rubrics ref, with `cancel-in-progress: false`, matching other stateful rubrics workflows. **Comments:** This is more important here because `/add-rubrics` is an explicit user-requested write path.
Commit/push failures can be summarized as no changes **Cause:** `steps.add_rubrics.outcome` can remain `success` even when the internal rubric commit step fails under `continue-on-error`, while `rubrics_committed` is simply false. **Candidate solutions:** Expose the rubric commit outcome from `run-agent-task` and pass it into `prepare-add-rubrics-summary`, or make commit/push failure force a failure summary for this route. **Comments:** Earlier stateful-failure feedback was partially fixed for early failures, but this persistence-failure case remains.
Prompt schema comment omits add-rubrics **Cause:** The new route was added to runtime validation and other docs, but the route-specific prompt’s YAML example was not updated. **Candidate solutions:** Add `add-rubrics` to the allowed `applies_to` route comment in `.github/prompts/agent-add-rubrics.md`. **Comments:** Both reviewer inputs identified this as a docs/prompt consistency issue.
## Recommended Next Step `FIX_PR`: the remaining findings are concrete, low-risk branch changes suitable for an automated fix-pr pass. ## Final Verdict `MINOR_ISSUES` ## Action Items - [ ] Add concurrency for `agent-add-rubrics.yml` so direct writes to the rubrics branch are serialized. - [ ] Make add-rubrics summaries surface rubric commit/push failures instead of reporting them as no committed changes. - [ ] Include `add-rubrics` in the prompt schema comment’s allowed `applies_to` route list.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 2/5).

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 93 | PARTIAL | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | The route is wired through dispatch policy and covered by a denial test for route-specific `AGENT_ACCESS_POLICY` overrides in `.agent/src/__tests__/triage.test.ts`; router dispatch occurs only after portal route resolution. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | `agent-add-rubrics.yml` reuses existing actions: auth resolution, provider resolution, runtime setup, `run-agent-task`, and `post-response`; summary formatting is added through the existing response/CLI pattern. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The PR matches issue #9’s plan: explicit `/add-rubrics`, direct `agent/rubrics` update, validation/commit through workflow plumbing, and in-thread summary. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | partial | 6/7 | Most route/docs lists were updated, but `.github/prompts/agent-add-rubrics.md` still omits `add-rubrics` from the schema comment’s allowed `applies_to` route list. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is scoped to the new route, workflow, prompt, summary formatter, focused tests, and matching docs. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | partial | 4/7 | Summary preparation now runs with `if: always()`, but rubric commit/push failures in `run-agent-task` are `continue-on-error`; the add-rubrics summary can still report “No changes were committed” while persistence actually failed. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Docs add route/workflow behavior and route lists without introducing file inventory tables. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation is minimal and follows existing route maps, workflow structure, formatter helpers, and tests. |

## Notes

- The main remaining rubric issues are prompt/docs consistency and stateful failure visibility for direct writes to `agent/rubrics`.
- GitHub currently reports PR #15 as `mergeable: CONFLICTING` / `mergeStateStatus: DIRTY`; that should be resolved before merge, but it is separate from the implementation-rubric score.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `.github/prompts/agent-add-rubrics.md` still excludes `add-rubrics` from its schema comment’s allowed `applies_to` routes, while runtime validation and other docs include it. Rubric: `keep-docs-in-sync`.
- **WARNING:** Rubric commit failures can be hidden from the user-facing add-rubrics summary because `Commit rubric edits` is `continue-on-error` and the summary only receives `steps.add_rubrics.outcome` plus `rubrics_committed`. Rubric: `surface-stateful-failures`.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-9/codex-25065166075`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

Resolved the PR merge conflicts by merging current `main` into the branch and keeping both route additions where they overlapped.

Conflict resolutions:
- `.agent/src/triage.ts`: explicit slash routes now include both `add-rubrics` and `orchestrate`.
- `AGENT.md`: direct response docs now list both `/add-rubrics` and `/orchestrate`.

Created merge commit:
`4d67038 Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/self-evolving/repo into agent/implement-issue-9/codex-25065166075`

Verification passed:
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/triage.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/response.test.js`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/rubrics.test.js`

Final status: working tree clean, and `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD FETCH_HEAD` reports a clean merge.

title: Add minimal add-rubrics route with dedicated workflow
